initial_draft:
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> I have 5 units of X and 25 units of Y. The other player (RED) proposes to give me 10 units of X in exchange for 10 units of Y. 
I do not have enough X to give if they wanted X from me, but they want to give me X. Wait, let's re-read. 
"Player RED Gives X: 10 | Player BLUE Gives Y: 10". 
So RED gives me 10 X. I give them 10 Y.
After this trade, I would have:
X: 5 + 10 = 15
Y: 25 - 10 = 15
This is a very favorable trade for me because I am giving away 10 units of a resource I have plenty of (Y) to acquire 10 units of a resource I have very little of (X). My X count would more than double, and my Y count would remain substantial. 
Since this is the last round, I should accept this good deal immediately to maximize my total resources.
Proposal count: 0. I am accepting, so I don't use a proposal. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> I accept your trade. It looks like a great deal for me. I will give you 10 Y and receive 10 X. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags are present in the correct order, and the content within them is valid and parseable.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. The trade gives me 10 X (increasing from 5 to 15) and costs me 10 Y (decreasing from 25 to 15). Since I value acquiring resources and X is currently scarce for me, this significantly advances my goal. No better split is plausible as it is the final round.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The trade is immediately accepted by me, so there is no risk of rejection from the opponent; the game ends successfully.
4. Consistency: OK. My reason explains the math and the logic behind the acceptance, which matches the "ACCEPT" answer and the "NONE" proposed trade. The message confirms the acceptance without contradicting the move.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I am accepting the trade, so I do not consume any of my 3 proposal limits. This is a valid move.
</critique>

iterations.refined:
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> I currently hold 5 units of X and 25 units of Y. The opponent proposes to give me 10 units of X in exchange for 10 units of Y. Executing this trade will result in me holding 15 units of X (5 + 10) and 15 units of Y (25 - 10). Since X is the scarce resource for me and Y is abundant, acquiring 10 X at the cost of 10 Y significantly improves my total resource count and diversity. Given that this is the final round, there is no strategic benefit to negotiating further or rejecting this offer to wait for a potentially worse counter-offer. Accepting immediately secures the best available outcome for this turn.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> I accept your offer. In exchange for 10 units of Y, I will receive 10 units of X. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
